description: Analytics Dashboard + Customer Feedback System for Tap2Dine restaurant SaaS

userStories.title: Add feedback table to database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a feedback table in SQLite so customer reviews can be stored persistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'feedback' table to database.py with columns: id (autoincrement), restaurant_id (text), table_no (text), rating (int 1-5), comment (text), created_at (timestamp default now)
Table is created in init_db() if it does not exist
db.add_feedback(data) method added to Database class
db.get_feedback(restaurant_id) method added returning list of feedback dicts
db.get_feedback_summary(restaurant_id) returns {avg_rating, count, recent: [last 5]}
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: database.py is at Tap2Dine/backend/database.py. Follow existing patterns for adding tables and methods.

userStories.title: Add revenue analytics to database layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need revenue calculation methods so the API can serve analytics data.
acceptanceCriteria: db.get_revenue_stats(restaurant_id) method added returning: total_revenue, revenue_today, revenue_this_week, revenue_this_month, daily_revenue (list of {date, revenue} for last 7 days)
Revenue is calculated from orders table using total field on delivered orders
db.get_peak_hours(restaurant_id) method added returning list of {hour: 0-23, count: int} for all orders
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Orders table already exists. Use SQL strftime for date grouping. Look at existing db.get_stats() for patterns.

userStories.title: Add feedback API endpoints
userStories.description: As a restaurant owner, I want to receive and view customer feedback via API.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/feedback endpoint added to main.py accepting {restaurant_id, table_no, rating (1-5), comment (optional)}
GET /api/feedback endpoint added with restaurant_id query param, returns {feedback: [], count: int}
GET /api/analytics/feedback-summary endpoint added returning {avg_rating, count, recent: []}
FeedbackCreate model added to models.py with validation (rating must be 1-5)
POST /api/feedback broadcasts 'new_feedback' event via WebSocket manager
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow exact same pattern as existing endpoints in main.py. Add model to models.py.

userStories.title: Add revenue analytics API endpoints
userStories.description: As a restaurant owner, I want to see revenue statistics and peak hours via API.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/analytics/revenue endpoint added with restaurant_id query param (default 'tap2dine')
Returns {total_revenue, revenue_today, revenue_this_week, revenue_this_month, daily_revenue: [{date, revenue}]}
GET /api/analytics/peak-hours endpoint added with restaurant_id query param
Returns {peak_hours: [{hour, count}], busiest_hour: int}
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use db.get_revenue_stats() and db.get_peak_hours() from US-002.

userStories.title: Add feedback widget to customer menu page
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to rate my dining experience from the menu page after ordering.
acceptanceCriteria: Add feedback section at bottom of Tap2Dine/index.html after the cart section
Section shows 5 clickable star icons (☆ / ★) for rating selection
Optional textarea for comment (placeholder: 'Tell us about your experience...')
Submit button posts to POST /api/feedback with current restaurant_id, table from URL, rating, comment
Show success message 'Thank you for your feedback! ⭐' after submission
Hide form after successful submission
Styling matches existing style.css design (dark theme, card-based)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: index.html uses vanilla JS. Restaurant ID and table number are read from URL params. Follow existing fetch() patterns.

userStories.title: Create analytics dashboard page
userStories.description: As a restaurant owner, I want a dedicated analytics page showing revenue, peak hours and customer feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: New file Tap2Dine/analytics.html created
Page has 4 revenue stat cards: Today / This Week / This Month / All Time (fetched from /api/analytics/revenue)
Page has a bar chart of peak hours using Chart.js from CDN (fetched from /api/analytics/peak-hours)
Page has feedback summary section: average star rating + total count + last 5 reviews as cards
Page has same header/nav as admin.html with a back link to admin.html
Page auto-refreshes data every 30 seconds
Page uses restaurant_id from URL param (default: tap2dine)
Dark theme matching admin.html / style.css
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow admin.html structure. Use Chart.js CDN: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js. Fetch all data on load.

userStories.title: Link analytics page from admin dashboard
userStories.description: As a restaurant owner, I want quick access to the analytics page from the admin panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Analytics' nav link in admin.html header nav pointing to analytics.html
Add an 'Analytics Dashboard' card/button in admin.html main content area with chart icon
Card shows today's revenue fetched from /api/analytics/revenue on page load
Clicking the card or nav link opens analytics.html
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: admin.html is at Tap2Dine/admin.html. Add to existing nav and stats grid.
